headline: Liftoff Mobile IPO: Why investors are comparing it to AppLovin
summary: Liftoff Mobile (LFTO) debuted on the Nasdaq Composite (^IXIC) on Thursday, raising $437 million and pricing the initial public offering (IPO) at $23 per share. Liftoff CEO Jeremy Bondy joins Yahoo Finance to explain why the IPO was delayed, discuss Liftoff's AI engine "Cortex," and to outline the differences between Liftoff and AppLovin (APP).

headline: AppLovin Rival Liftoff Stock Jumps After IPO
summary: Liftoff Mobile — a potential rival to AppLovin in the mobile advertising tech space — completed an initial public offering Thursday, selling 19 million shares at 23, above its 2o to 22 range.  The response highlights investor appetite for new offerings, with the much-anticipated SpaceX IPO expected next week. Shares of quantum computing player Quantinuum climbed as much as 18% in its debut Thursday before retreating.  Liftoff stock rose 19.6% to close at 27.51 on the stock market today.

headline: Trade Desk Is Down 44% This Year and AppLovin Is Down 17%. Are Ad-Tech Stocks Dead Money in 2026?
summary: Ad-tech investors are staring at a tough tape in 2026 so far. Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD) trades near $21, while AppLovin (NASDAQ:APP) sits around $561 as some loyal shareholders start to lose patience. The headline numbers tell the story. headline: Top Growth Companies With Insider Ownership In June 2026
summary: Over the last 7 days, the United States market has remained flat, though it has seen a significant rise of 26% over the past year, with earnings forecasted to grow by 17% annually. In this environment, growth companies with high insider ownership can be particularly appealing as they often indicate strong confidence from those who know the business best.

headline: 8x8, BlackLine, and AppLovin Stocks Trade Down, What You Need To Know
summary: A number of stocks fell in the afternoon session after software stocks declined for a second consecutive session, extending the profit-taking that began earlier in the week.
